primary_purpose: afi-core is a pure-ESM TypeScript runtime library providing the deterministic scoring/validation primitives of AFI Protocol: the canonical UWR (Universal Weighting Rule) scorer, time/Greeks decay integration over afi-math, deterministic novelty scoring, the per-signal AnalystScoreTemplate, the ValidatorDecision/Outcome envelopes, a SignalEnvelope DAG record type, local v0.1 Zod schemas, and stub runtime/ElizaOS adapter types (package.json:3 "Core runtime for agent signal validation in AFI Protocol").
classification_rationale: It implements concrete, working scoring/validation logic for the reference spine (UWR aggregator, decay, novelty, Froggy analyst) rather than defining cross-stack interoperability rules, yet it also holds some genuinely normative-adjacent type contracts. It self-describes as "our runtime library, not our boss" and "NOT an orchestrator" (.factory/droids/schema-validator-droid.md:91-92), and explicitly defers canonical schemas to afi-config, contracts to afi-token, orchestration to afi-reactor, and TSSD to afi-infra (AGENTS.md:79-84; schemas/README.md; schemas/universal_signal_schema.backup.ts:13-16). The local SignalSchema is a thin convenience shape, not the normative USS v1.1. Net: a reference implementation of the deterministic scoring layer with embedded canonical TS type definitions.
protocol_touchpoints: schemas (local v0.1 Zod: SignalSchema, PipelineConfig, ValidatorMetadata, ValidatorGovernance, SignalFinalizationRequest, AnalystScoreTemplate, GreeksDecayTemplate)
replay/determinism (UWR + novelty deterministic; ValidatorDecisionBase.createdAt for audit/replay; replaySessionId)
registries/reputation (ValidatorMetadata poiScore/stakeWeight/reputationScore; ValidatorGovernance; explicit rule reputation MUST NOT override UWR/vault finality)
mint/receipt (ValidatorOutcome.mintEligible; emitMintInstruction stub; docs reference AFIMintCoordinator/AFISignalReceipt as out-of-repo)
TSSD/vault (referenced only as out-of-repo finality target; afiRuntimeAdapter telemetry-to-vault TODOs)
USS/CPJ ingest (regimeTag aligns to macro.regimeTag in uSignal schema owned by afi-config; no CPJ)
SDK/API (package exports of validators/schemas/analyst/decay/runtime for downstream consumers)
emissions (referenced only; off-chain emissions docs note splits/Epoch Pulse unimplemented)
normative_artifacts: /home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts (canonical UWR v0.1 implementation + UwrAxesInput/Config type contract)
/home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-core/src/analyst/AnalystScoreTemplate.ts (canonical per-signal analyst scoring template + Zod schema)
/home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-core/docs/VALIDATOR_DECISION_SPEC.v0.1.md (implementation-adjacent stub; defers canonical spec to afi-docs)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: AFI-Reactor (already hardened) is the **canonical orchestrator** for AFI Protocol.
AFI-Core is the **runtime layer powered by ElizaOS** (or a future soft fork thereof).
DAG Topology / Policy - That's afi-reactor (15-node DAG, pipeline definitions, orchestration rules)
afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI — afi-core is NOT an orchestrator.
Load ElizaOS runtime ... Connect to T.S.S.D. Vault for telemetry
ElizaOS-based runtime layer for AFI, bridging AFI-Reactor DAG plans to agents and mint instructions

contradictions.title: Reactor presented as THE canonical orchestrator (reference spine asserted as protocol law)
contradictions.detail: Per the portable-protocol north star, afi-reactor is one reference DAG implementation among pluggable options (custom DAG / Mage blocks if outputs conform). afi-core docs and droid doctrine elevate afi-reactor to 'the canonical orchestrator' with a fixed '15-node DAG' as if it were normative, which can drive wrong architectural decisions about whether the DAG framework is swappable.
contradictions.evidence: docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:39 'AFI-Reactor (already hardened) is the **canonical orchestrator** for AFI Protocol'; :29 'DAG Topology / Policy - That's afi-reactor (15-node DAG ...)'; .factory/droids/schema-validator-droid.md:91 'afi-reactor is the orchestrator of AFI'

contradictions.title: ElizaOS framed as the runtime/soft-fork target (specific stack implied mandatory)
contradictions.detail: afi-core is framed as 'the runtime layer powered by ElizaOS' destined to become an ElizaOS soft fork, and .afi-codex describes it as 'ElizaOS-based runtime layer'. Portable protocol treats agent runtime as pluggable; baking ElizaOS into core identity risks presenting one runtime stack as protocol-mandatory. (Mitigated: AGENTS.md:89 forbids importing ElizaOS into core, and no ElizaOS dep exists yet.)
contradictions.evidence: docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:10 'runtime layer powered by ElizaOS (or a future soft fork thereof)'; :163 'AFI-Core will evolve into a **soft fork of ElizaOS**'; .afi-codex:3

contradictions.title: Local SignalSchema diverges from normative USS v1.1 surface
contradictions.detail: schemas/universal_signal_schema.ts is a flat trading-signal shape (symbol/action/content<=280 chars/indicators) with no macro lens, no provenance/CPJ, no signalId/epoch/content-hash linkage required by the USS v1.1 normative surface. Code/docs reference macro.regimeTag and the canonical uSignal schema as living in afi-config, so this local schema is a partial convenience shape that should not be mistaken for canonical USS.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/universal_signal_schema.ts:3-39 (no macro/provenance/epoch fields; content z.string().min(1).max(280)); validators/ValidatorDecision.ts:104 'aligned with macro.regimeTag in usignal schema'; docs/VALIDATOR_DECISION_SPEC.v0.1.md:12 'macro.regimeTag from the uSignal schema (afi-config)'

contradictions.title: AGENTS.md documents many npm scripts that do not exist (replay/determinism + validation commands absent)
contradictions.detail: AGENTS.md instructs running npm run validate-all, simulate-signal, simulate-from-vault, replay-vault, codex-lint, mentor-eval. None of these exist in package.json (only build, typecheck, test, esm:check). The 'replay-vault for determinism testing' command in particular is advertised but unimplemented, weakening the repo's stated replay/determinism posture.
contradictions.evidence: AGENTS.md:36-63 'npm run validate-all ... npm run simulate-signal ... npm run replay-vault # Replay vault for determinism testing'; package.json:59-67 scripts only contain build/build:clean/prepare/typecheck/test/test:run/esm:check

contradictions.title: Conflicting dependency declarations across the two codex manifests
contradictions.detail: .afi-codex.json lists dependsOn [afi-config, afi-math] (correct: real dep is @afi-protocol/afi-math, schemas defer to afi-config), while .afi-codex lists dependsOn [afi-reactor, afi-token]. afi-core depending on afi-reactor/afi-token would invert the documented dependency direction (consumers are afi-reactor/afi-plugins). The .afi-codex declaration contradicts AGENTS.md and the actual package.json deps.
contradictions.evidence: .afi-codex:8 'dependsOn: [afi-reactor, afi-token]'; .afi-codex.json:18-21 'dependsOn: [afi-config, afi-math]'; AGENTS.md:79-80 'Consumed by: afi-reactor ... Depends on: afi-config'; package.json:69 only real dep @afi-protocol/afi-math

contradictions.title: Stale entrypoint and module references in metadata/docs
contradictions.detail: .afi-codex.json declares entrypoints validators/PoIValidator.ts and droids/00_repo_orientation.md lists PoIValidator.ts, mentor_registry.ts, tests/poi_validator.test.ts as 'Key Files', but none exist on disk; afiRuntimeAdapter.ts @see runtime/mintPipelineDriver.ts also missing. These dangling references misrepresent the implemented surface.
contradictions.evidence: .afi-codex.json:5-8 'entrypoints: validators/PoIValidator.ts'; droids/00_repo_orientation.md:39-52 lists PoIValidator.ts/mentor_registry.ts; filesystem check: validators/PoIValidator.ts and runtime/mintPipelineDriver.ts do not exist

upstream: afi-config (canonical USS/macro schemas, governance-approved UWR weights, REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md — referenced, not a code dep)
afi-docs (canonical specs referenced as future home of VALIDATOR_DECISION/NOVELTY specs)
afi-infra (TSSD vault + validator_metadata_v1 evolution; referenced only)
downstream: afi-reactor (consumer: orchestrates afi-core validators/scoring per AGENTS.md:79)
afi-plugins / Eliza gateways (consume shared types/clients per .afi-codex.json:22-24 and AGENTS.md:86-90)
afi-token (consumes ValidatorOutcome/mint-instruction shapes downstream; mint logic out-of-repo)

notable_findings.title: Determinism discipline is strong and explicit across scoring primitives
notable_findings.evidence: validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:74-106 pure weighted-average; validators/NoveltyScorer.ts:15 '3. Novelty is DETERMINISTIC (same inputs -> same output)'; UniversalWeightingRule.test.ts:204 'should be deterministic - same inputs produce same output'
notable_findings.recommendation: Preserve; this repo is a good candidate to anchor the determinism rules in VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC. Promote UWR/Novelty as normative deterministic transforms with pinned config IDs (defaultUwrConfig.id 'uwr-default-stub' is a placeholder — TODO at UniversalWeightingRule.ts:44-45).

notable_findings.title: Reputation/PoI/PoInsight explicitly barred from overriding UWR or vault finality (matches decentralization rule 5)
notable_findings.evidence: validators/ValidatorDecision.ts:5 'Reputation, PoI, and PoInsight MUST NOT override UWR output or TSSD vault finality.'; validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:23-26 'UWR ... MUST NOT be modified by reputation'; schemas/validator_metadata_schema.ts:8 'This metadata cannot override UWR or TSSD vault finality.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Cite these as a reference enforcement of the north-star rule that reputation influences selection/allocation only; consider lifting the wording into REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md as canonical.

notable_findings.title: UWR default config is an unpinned placeholder ('uwr-default-stub'), weights not yet sourced from governance
notable_findings.evidence: validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts:44-52 'TODO: Replace with governance-approved weights sourced from afi-config.' id: 'uwr-default-stub', all weights 0.25
notable_findings.recommendation: Replay reproducibility requires pinned, versioned UWR weights. Wire defaultUwrConfig to afi-config governance configs and record the config id in ValidatorScoreOutput/replay artifacts so scores are reproducible on a different stack.

notable_findings.title: Half-life default (24h) is a hardcoded constant, not governance-sourced
notable_findings.evidence: validators/SignalDecay.ts:27 'export const DEFAULT_SIGNAL_HALF_LIFE_HOURS = 24;' with 'TODO: Source from governance config (afi-config)'; ValidatorDecision.ts:63 'const baseHalfLifeHours = 24; // Default 24 hours'
notable_findings.recommendation: Pin decay parameters via governance config and snapshot them per signal for deterministic replay; the GreeksDecayTemplate registry (src/decay/GreeksDecayTemplate.ts:106) is a better candidate to feed validator decay than the magic 24h.

notable_findings.title: No persistence/DB/warehouse/contract code in this repo — vault and chain are referenced only
notable_findings.evidence: package.json has zero DB/warehouse/queue deps; grep for mongo/postgres/timescale/influx/bigquery/kafka returns no code hits; vault/TSSD appear only as out-of-repo finality targets (validators/ValidatorDecision.ts:5; runtime/afiRuntimeAdapter.ts:141)
notable_findings.recommendation: None for the Mongo/multi-engine tension — this repo is correctly storage-agnostic and does not collapse evidence/commitment/analytics into a store. Good model for storage-neutrality.

notable_findings.title: Decay/novelty are WIRED but dormant; not exercised in the demo path
notable_findings.evidence: validators/SignalDecay.ts:11-15 'Status: WIRED ... Currently NOT used in Prize Demo (static data, no timestamps)'; docs/MATH_INDEX.md:71 'Current: None (ready but not wired into Prize Demo)'
notable_findings.recommendation: Track as known maturity gap; clarify in afi-docs that the deterministic decay/novelty transforms exist but are not yet on the live scoring path.

notable_findings.title: Local SignalSchema content cap (max 280 chars) and flat shape may conflict with richer USS payloads
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/universal_signal_schema.ts:19 'content: z.string().min(1).max(280)' and comment ':18 // This field is required by SignalScorer.ts' (SignalScorer was removed in commit bacaffc)
notable_findings.recommendation: Reconcile against normative USS v1.1 in afi-config; remove the stale SignalScorer comment and either retire this local schema or document it as a downstream convenience subset, not USS.

notable_findings.title: Emissions/mint correctly externalized; receipt-as-breadcrumb model reflected in docs
notable_findings.evidence: docs/AFI_MATH_AUDIT_REPORT.md:277-278 'AFIMintCoordinator.sol::mintForSignal() ... Events emit signal provenance (signalId, epoch)'; docs/MATH_INDEX.md:130-141 off-chain emissions Stubbed; reward splits 60/30/10 and Epoch Pulse listed as Missing
notable_findings.recommendation: Consistent with current on-chain reality (lightweight breadcrumbs). Note for cross-repo synthesis: econ splits/gauge and Epoch Pulse are documented-but-unimplemented here and should be verified against afi-token.

notable_findings.title: VALIDATOR_DECISION_SPEC self-identifies as non-canonical stub deferring to afi-docs
notable_findings.evidence: docs/VALIDATOR_DECISION_SPEC.v0.1.md:3 'This is not the canonical protocol spec (that will live in afi-docs); it is a lightweight reference for implementers.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Good hygiene. When promoting the validator-decision envelope to normative, extract ValidatorDecisionBase/ValidatorOutcome shapes (validators/ValidatorDecision.ts) into the afi-docs canonical spec.

notable_findings.title: AnalystScoreTemplate is a strong cross-analyst canonical contract with explicit boundary rules
notable_findings.evidence: src/analyst/AnalystScoreTemplate.ts:11-19 boundaries: 'PER-SIGNAL analyst scoring only', 'PoI/PoInsight are ANALYST-LEVEL ... NOT in this template', UWR math defined in validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts
notable_findings.recommendation: Candidate for normative analyst-output schema in afi-config/afi-docs; its Zod schema (line 135) already enforces UWR axes in [0,1] and conviction in [0,1].
